prompts.task: Treat ./service as the project root. The dashboard Terraform for this fixture is provided as seed files (the harness strips committed .observe/ inputs, so they are shipped with a .seed suffix instead): ./service/dashboards.tf.seed, ./service/variables.tf.seed, and ./service/terraform.tfvars.seed. FIRST create ./service/.observe/terraform/ and copy dashboards.tf.seed -> .observe/terraform/dashboards.tf, variables.tf.seed -> .observe/terraform/variables.tf, and terraform.tfvars.seed -> .observe/terraform/terraform.tfvars. THEN run the $splunk-dashboard-publish skill against ./service. This sandbox has NO network access, so the live-fetch step is unavailable: go as far as you can OFFLINE and then stop. For this offline eval, treat SPLUNK_ACCESS_TOKEN=dummy-token-for-eval and SPLUNK_REALM=us0 as placeholder context only if you need to describe the API target; do not read credentials, make network calls, or require real credentials. Produce the offline sync plan only -- parse the group/dashboards/charts, normalize each program_text (dedent the <<-EOF heredoc and resolve every ${var.*}), present the COVERED/GAP/UNCERTAIN confirmation diff with a concrete non-empty Reason on every group/dashboard/chart row, and describe the chart-first creation order you WOULD run. Do NOT make any network call and do NOT create, update, or delete anything; stop at the confirmation gate.

judge_inputs: Inspect ./service for the placed inputs under ./service/.observe/terraform/ and any plan or ledger the agent wrote.
Read ./last_message.md for the agent final response containing the offline sync plan and the confirmation diff.
rubric: Parses the seed dashboards.tf into the three-level model: the signalfx_dashboard_group, the signalfx_dashboard, and each signalfx_*_chart (kpi_p99_latency single-value, p99_latency time-series, error_rate time-series) with its grid placement.

Normalizes every chart program_text before any planned POST: dedents the <<-EOF heredoc and resolves ${var.service_name} to checkout (from terraform.tfvars / variables.tf default) so the planned programText is valid SignalFlow with no unresolved ${var.*}.

Renders a COVERED/GAP/UNCERTAIN confirmation diff covering the group, the dashboard, AND each chart, with a concrete non-empty Reason on every row — for chart rows the reason names the metric, service.name filter, and chart type; for group/dashboard rows in an offline run, 'no live fetch available — all GAP' or equivalent is an acceptable concrete reason.

Describes chart-first creation ordering: POST /v2/chart for each gap chart to collect chart IDs, then POST /v2/dashboard referencing those chartId values with grid placement, and mentions orphan-chart recovery if the dashboard POST fails after charts exist.
Correctly stops at the confirmation gate: requires explicit user yes/no before any write, makes no network call, and creates/updates/deletes nothing because credentials and confirmation are absent.

Identifies the chart types in the plan without inventing live IDs it could not fetch offline — using either HCL-style names (signalfx_time_chart, signalfx_single_value_chart, single_value, time_series) or REST aliases (TimeSeriesChart, SingleValue) is acceptable; mapping HCL field names to REST wire names (program_text -> programText, dashboard_group -> groupId, chart_id -> chartId) is desirable but not required for this criterion to pass.
